modules.scope: Canonical regular-grid planning, exact order and twist maps, provider Born-von Karman cache hits and fail-closed invalidation passed forward and reverse dense-oracle checks. CP2K still recalibrates symmetry phases during each map preparation, so this is not a claim of a fully persistent CP2K phase cache.
The reduced forward transaction does not retain full-k density or overlap input arrays and reproduces the dense oracle through 9x9x1, including permutations and negative recovery paths.

The provider complex AO-image workspace obeys the checked fixed-batch high-water formula in 39 derivative evaluations. This does not imply k-independent total CP2K memory because irreducible CP2K matrices and immutable metadata remain resident.

Streamed overlap adjoints, direct force derivatives, and direct stress derivatives passed the dense oracle for RKS, UKS, 1D, 2D, 3D, full, time-reversal, K290, SPGLIB, and shifted meshes at MPI P=1,2,4.
Selectable forward and reverse consumers expand one star block transiently and fold each response immediately. Qualification modes retain the complete-mesh oracle by design.
Physical overlap expansion, anti-linear time reversal, K290/SPGLIB rotation, and weighted real-adjoint foldback reproduce full-array operators and final energy, force, and stress observables.
modules.current_evidence: The once-per-map helper qualification enforces physical-overlap expansion, a deterministic complex-Hermitian per-star weighted real-adjoint identity including negative rotp, and blockwise fold identity against the unchanged full-array oracle. Five current/baseline pairs (ten zero-returncode runs) cover K290 and SPGLIB 2x2x2 plus 3x1x1 time reversal, including analytical and finite-difference derivatives.

modules.scope: The factorized direct DFT backend is selectable and matches the dense transform for energy, Fock response, forces, and stress. It is not an FFT and retains direct-transform arithmetic.
No FFT backend exists. Dense and separable direct transforms remain the oracle and optimized direct paths.

Official CP2K k-point-group ownership, exact star coverage, and reduced-result foldback pass the full oracle including P greater than nred. The nonlinear provider kernel remains single-state in this precursor.
Distributed pre-kernel k-to-R source formation with root application passed 20 dense-versus-partial pairs and five fail-closed fault hooks. Kernel execution and R-to-k pulls remain root-serial.

modules.evidence: Provider API and provider-only tests are archived; CP2K integration is under qualification.
modules.scope: The planned intermediate path replicates the nonlinear kernel but assigns disjoint full-k R-to-k pulls, including empty importer ownership when P exceeds Nk. It is not distributed-kernel scaling.
A genuinely distributed nonlinear exchange kernel and strong/weak scaling evidence do not yet exist.

The opt-in VALIDATED_BVK_TRANSFER initial guess is transactionally fail-closed and passed RKS, UKS, MPI, force, and stress tests. It is not the default and does not guarantee positivity or N-representability.
ACP forward and gradient paths still materialize complete-mesh projector or density arrays and require a separate streamed support-plan implementation.
The remaining mixer covariance qualifier allocates a complete Fock mesh; a streamed selector with the dense qualifier retained as oracle is under implementation.
